media_processing_validation_error: Fix the media processing rules before saving.
media_processing_editor_desc: Enable the processors you want, bind extensions when needed, and let the AsterDrive built-in pipeline handle the final fallback when it is enabled.
media_processing_editor_validation_title: Media processing config has validation issues
media_processing_editor_processor_extens: Only matching files will be routed into this processor.
media_processing_editor_processor_builti: AsterDrive built-in processing pipeline. It does not need a command, and it participates in the listed capabilities when enabled.
The AsterDrive built-in processor does not need a command. Once disabled, it no longer participates in its media processing capabilities.
AsterDrive built-in image pipeline for common image thumbnails and image metadata fallback. It does not need a command.
The image built-in pipeline handles AsterDrive's own image thumbnails and image metadata. When disabled, images rely on enabled external CLI processors first.
AsterDrive built-in audio pipeline for audio artwork thumbnails and audio metadata fallback. It does not need a command.
The audio built-in pipeline reads artwork and metadata from audio tags. When disabled, audio capabilities only use other enabled processors.
media_processing_editor_processor_vips_d: Uses VIPS CLI for image thumbnails, especially formats the built-in path may not cover well, such as HEIC, AVIF, TIFF, and RAW.
media_processing_editor_processor_ffmpeg: Uses FFmpeg CLI to capture video thumbnails for formats such as MP4, MOV, MKV, and WebM.
media_processing_editor_processor_ffprob: Uses FFprobe CLI to read video media information. It handles video metadata only and does not generate thumbnails.
media_processing_editor_processor_comman: Executable name or absolute path used when running this CLI processor. Only enable it when this command is available on the server.
media_processing_editor_processor_vips_c: Command used to run the VIPS image processor. Enable it only when VIPS is installed and executable on the server.
media_processing_editor_processor_ffmpeg: Command used to run the FFmpeg video thumbnail processor. Enable it only when FFmpeg is installed and executable on the server.
media_processing_editor_processor_ffprob: Command used to run the FFprobe video metadata processor. It is usually installed with FFmpeg, but this command is tested separately.
media_processing_editor_rule_extensions_: Comma-separated suffixes like `heic, heif`. Leave empty to match any supported file.
Only matching image suffixes use VIPS. Add formats where the built-in image path is not enough.
Only matching video suffixes use FFmpeg to capture thumbnails.
Only matching video suffixes use FFprobe to parse duration, codec, and other media information.
media_processing_error_parse: Media processing config must be valid JSON.
media_processing_error_version_mismatch: Media processing config version must be {{version}}.
media_processing_error_no_enabled_proces: Enable at least one media processor.
